_comment: Coder-Next coprocessor with (a) text/coding POOLED across Mac (MLX) + box (qwentescence, llama.cpp >= b9596), and (b) a multimodal backend for vision/OCR (Coder-Next is text-only). Copy to ~/.qwen-coprocessor-stack/config.json. Routing (src/backends.ts): heavy/coding prompts -> weighted round-robin across the two 'heavy' text backends (the Coder-Next pool, both machines used simultaneously); qwen_oneshot_vision -> the 'multimodal' backend; qwen_embed/qwen_rerank -> their modality. ROLES (bead k8j): qwen_chat opts.role routes by the 'roles' array — role='code' -> the Coder-Next pool (fast, bulk operator dispatch), role='general'/'reasoning' -> the 35B (slower but reasons; it's the vision-box model, reached via role even though vision_only keeps it out of the agentic text pool). See tests/routing-pool-vision.test.ts.

backends._comment: Coder-Next on the Mac (MLX/Metal, M4 Max). Higher weight = faster host gets more of the pool.

Coder-Next on the box (qwentescence, llama.cpp b9596 Vulkan). no_agentic (bead 081): EXCLUDED from the agentic pool (qwen_spawn/oneshot) because it reliably CRASHES on the qwen-code agentic request shape (qwen3_next Gated-Delta-Net Vulkan; not fixed by cache-reuse/kv-unified/reboot/b9611). Still serves DIRECT qwen_chat (role=code) + tokenize fine. So agentic coding goes to coder-mac; coder-box does fast direct operator dispatch.

qwen3.6-35B-A3B with mmproj. Serves TWO explicit paths on one loaded model: (1) qwen_oneshot_vision by modality=multimodal; (2) qwen_chat role='general'/'reasoning' by the roles array (a general/reasoning instruct model — beats Coder-Next on judgment/reasoning operator tasks, slower). vision_only keeps it out of the agentic text pool (qwen_spawn/oneshot); role routing reaches it regardless. See bead k8j.
